stem: Which of these is an example of OUTPUT from a computer?

options.text: Pressing a key on the keyboard

explanation_after: Output is what the computer sends back to you after processing. Text on screen, sound from speakers, and files saved to disk are all outputs. Keyboard and mouse are inputs — they send information TO the computer.

stem: A language model like GPT takes text as input and produces text as output. In a voice assistant, what is the INPUT and what is the OUTPUT?

options.text: Input: the speaker / Output: the microphone
Input: your spoken words (via microphone) / Output: spoken response (via speaker)
Input: the AI model / Output: the internet

explanation_after: Every AI system is fundamentally an input-output machine. Voice → speech recognition converts audio to text (input). The model processes text and generates a response. Text-to-speech converts that back to audio (output). Understanding this pipeline is how you reason about any AI system.

prompt: A keyboard is an ______ device. A monitor (screen) is an ______ device.
explanation_after: Input devices send data to the computer. Output devices receive data from the computer. Some devices are both — a touchscreen is input (you touch it) and output (it displays things).
